sourceQuestion:
A router uses the following routing table:

Destination      | Mask            | Interface
144.16.0.0       | 255.255.0.0     | eth0
144.16.64.0      | 255.255.255.0   | eth1
144.16.68.0      | 255.255.255.0   | eth2
144.16.68.64     | 255.255.255.0   | eth3

A packet whose destination address is 144.16.68.117 arrives at the router. On which interface will the packet be forwarded?

A. eth0  B. eth1  C. eth2  D. eth3

parsed.stem:
A router uses the following routing table:

Destination      | Mask            | Interface
144.16.0.0       | 255.255.0.0     | eth0
144.16.64.0      | 255.255.255.0   | eth1
144.16.68.0      | 255.255.255.0   | eth2
144.16.68.64     | 255.255.255.0   | eth3

A packet whose destination address is 144.16.68.117 arrives at the router. On which interface will the packet be forwarded?

sourceQuestion:
A subnetted Class B network has the following subnet broadcast address: 144.16.95.255. Its subnet mask
A. is necessarily 255.255.224.0
B. is necessarily 255.255.240.0
C. is necessarily 255.255.248.0
D. could be any one of 255.255.224.0, 255.255.240.0, 255.255.248.0

sourceQuestion:
Host X has IP address 192.168.1.97 and is connected through two routers R1 and R2 to another host Y with IP address 192.168.1.110. R1 has IP addresses 192.168.1.135 and 192.168.1.110. R2 has IP addresses 192.168.1.67 and 192.168.1.155. The netmask used in the network is 255.255.255.224.

Given the information above, how many distinct subnets are guaranteed to already exist in the network?
A. 1
B. 2
C. 3
D. 6

parsed.stem:
Host X has IP address 192.168.1.97 and is connected through two routers R1 and R2 to another host Y with IP address 192.168.1.110. R1 has IP addresses 192.168.1.135 and 192.168.1.110. R2 has IP addresses 192.168.1.67 and 192.168.1.155. The netmask used in the network is 255.255.255.224.

Given the information above, how many distinct subnets are guaranteed to already exist in the network?

sourceQuestion:
Host X has IP address 192.168.1.97 and is connected through two routers R1 and R2 to another host Y with IP address 192.168.1.110. R1 has IP addresses 192.168.1.135 and 192.168.1.110. R2 has IP addresses 192.168.1.67 and 192.168.1.155. The netmask used in the network is 255.255.255.224.

Which IP address should host X configure as its gateway?
A. 192.168.1.67
B. 192.168.1.97
C. 192.168.1.135
D. 192.168.1.155

Which IP address should host X configure as its gateway?

sourceQuestion:
While opening a TCP connection, the initial sequence number is to be derived using a time-of-day (ToD) clock that keeps running even when the host is down. The low order 32 bits of the counter of the ToD clock is to be used for the initial sequence numbers. The clock counter increments once per millisecond. The maximum packet lifetime is given to be 64s.

Which one of the choices given is closest to the minimum permissible rate at which sequence numbers used for packets in a connection must be incremented?
A. 0.015/s
B. 0.064/s
C. 0.135/s
D. 0.327/s

parsed.stem:
While opening a TCP connection, the initial sequence number is to be derived using a time-of-day (ToD) clock that keeps running even when the host is down. The low order 32 bits of the counter of the ToD clock is to be used for the initial sequence numbers. The clock counter increments once per millisecond. The maximum packet lifetime is given to be 64s.

Which one of the choices given is closest to the minimum permissible rate at which sequence numbers used for packets in a connection must be incremented?

sourceQuestion:
Which of the following transport layer protocols is used to support electronic mail?

A. SMTP  B. IP  C. TCP  D. UDP

parsed.stem: Which of the following transport layer protocols is used to support electronic mail?

sourceQuestion:
Suppose two hosts use a TCP connection to transfer a large file. Which of the following statements is/are FALSE with respect to the TCP connection?

I. The sequence number of a segment is the sequence number of the first byte of data in the segment; the sequence number of the next segment is always mn + 1, where mn is the sequence number of the current segment.
II. If the estimated round trip time at any given point of time is f sec, the value of the retransmission timeout is always set to greater than or equal to f sec.
III. The number of unacknowledged bytes at the sender is always less than or equal to the receiver's advertised window.
IV. The number of unacknowledged bytes at the sender is always less than or equal to the minimum of the advertised window and the congestion window.

A. III only  B. I and III only  C. I and IV only  D. II and IV only

parsed.stem:
Suppose two hosts use a TCP connection to transfer a large file. Which of the following statements is/are FALSE with respect to the TCP connection?

I. The sequence number of a segment is the sequence number of the first byte of data in the segment; the sequence number of the next segment is always mn + 1, where mn is the sequence number of the current segment.
II. If the estimated round trip time at any given point of time is f sec, the value of the retransmission timeout is always set to greater than or equal to f sec.
III. The number of unacknowledged bytes at the sender is always less than or equal to the receiver's advertised window.
IV. The number of unacknowledged bytes at the sender is always less than or equal to the minimum of the advertised window and the congestion window.

sourceQuestion:
Consider a link between a TCP client and a TCP server with the following specifications: round trip time = 6 ms, slow-start threshold at the start = 32 KB, and the maximum segment size (MSS) = 2 KB. Assume that there are no timeouts and errors during transmission. Then the size of the congestion window (in KB) at time t = 60 ms after all acknowledgements are processed is ___.

A. 7 MSS  B. 8 MSS  C. 14 MSS  D. 16 MSS

parsed.stem: Consider a link between a TCP client and a TCP server with the following specifications: round trip time = 6 ms, slow-start threshold at the start = 32 KB, and the maximum segment size (MSS) = 2 KB. Assume that there are no timeouts and errors during transmission. Then the size of the congestion window (in KB) at time t = 60 ms after all acknowledgements are processed is ___.

sourceQuestion:
Consider the following statements:
I. TCP connections are full duplex
II. TCP has no option for selective acknowledgement
III. Only II and III are correct
IV. All of I, II, and III are correct

Which of the following is correct?

A. Only I is correct  B. Only I and II are correct  C. Only II and III are correct  D. All of I, II and III are correct

parsed.stem:
Consider the following statements:
I. TCP connections are full duplex
II. TCP has no option for selective acknowledgement
III. Only II and III are correct
IV. All of I, II, and III are correct

Which of the following is correct?

options.text: Only I and II are correct
Only II and III are correct
All of I, II and III are correct

sourceQuestion:
Identify the correct sequence in which the following packets are transmitted on the network by a host when a browser requests a webpage from a remote server, assuming that the DNS cache and the browser cache are both empty.

A. HTTP GET, DNS query, TCP SYN, HTTP GET  B. DNS query, HTTP GET, TCP SYN, HTTP GET  C. DNS query, TCP SYN, HTTP GET  D. TCP SYN, DNS query, HTTP GET
sourceAnswer: C. DNS query, TCP SYN, HTTP GET

parsed.stem: Identify the correct sequence in which the following packets are transmitted on the network by a host when a browser requests a webpage from a remote server, assuming that the DNS cache and the browser cache are both empty.

options.text: HTTP GET, DNS query, TCP SYN, HTTP GET
DNS query, HTTP GET, TCP SYN, HTTP GET
DNS query, TCP SYN, HTTP GET
TCP SYN, DNS query, HTTP GET

sourceQuestion:
Consider a TCP client and a TCP server running on two different machines. After completing data transfer, the TCP client calls close to terminate the connection and a FIN segment is sent to the TCP server. Server-side TCP responds by sending an ACK, which is received by the client-side TCP. As per the TCP connection state diagram (RFC 793), in which state does the client-side TCP connection wait for the FIN from the server-side TCP?

A. LAST-ACK
B. TIME-WAIT
C. FIN-WAIT-1
D. FIN-WAIT-2

parsed.stem: Consider a TCP client and a TCP server running on two different machines. After completing data transfer, the TCP client calls close to terminate the connection and a FIN segment is sent to the TCP server. Server-side TCP responds by sending an ACK, which is received by the client-side TCP. As per the TCP connection state diagram (RFC 793), in which state does the client-side TCP connection wait for the FIN from the server-side TCP?

sourceQuestion:
A server application is programmed to listen on port number P on host H. A client is connected to the server over the network. Consider that while the connection was active, the server machine crashed and rebooted. Assume that the client does not use the keepalive timer. Which of the following behaviors is correct?

A. If the client sends a packet, it will receive a packet indicating it needs to wait
B. If the client sends a packet after reboot, it will receive a RST segment
C. If the client sends a packet after reboot, it will receive a FIN segment
D. The sequence number N_Q is equal to N_P + 1
sourceAnswer: B. If the client sends a packet after reboot, it will receive a RST segment

parsed.stem: A server application is programmed to listen on port number P on host H. A client is connected to the server over the network. Consider that while the connection was active, the server machine crashed and rebooted. Assume that the client does not use the keepalive timer. Which of the following behaviors is correct?

options.text: If the client sends a packet, it will receive a packet indicating it needs to wait
If the client sends a packet after reboot, it will receive a RST segment
If the client sends a packet after reboot, it will receive a FIN segment
The sequence number N_Q is equal to N_P + 1

sourceQuestion:
Consider two hosts P and Q connected through a router R. The maximum transfer unit (MTU) value of the link between P and R is 1500 bytes, and between R and Q is 820 bytes. A TCP segment of size 1800 bytes was transferred from P to Q through R, with identification value as 0x1234. Assume that the IP header size is 20 bytes. Further, the packet is allowed to be fragmented. Which of the following statements is/are correct?

A. Two fragments are created at R and the IP header of the second fragment has 'Don't Fragment' (DF) bit set to 1
B. Two fragments are created at R and the IP header of the second fragment has identification 0x1234
C. Three fragments are created at R and the IP header of the second fragment has identification 0x1234
D. Three fragments are created at R and the IP header of the third fragment has 'More Fragment' (MF) bit set to 0

parsed.stem: Consider two hosts P and Q connected through a router R. The maximum transfer unit (MTU) value of the link between P and R is 1500 bytes, and between R and Q is 820 bytes. A TCP segment of size 1800 bytes was transferred from P to Q through R, with identification value as 0x1234. Assume that the IP header size is 20 bytes. Further, the packet is allowed to be fragmented. Which of the following statements is/are correct?

options.text: Two fragments are created at R and the IP header of the second fragment has 'Don't Fragment' (DF) bit set to 1
Two fragments are created at R and the IP header of the second fragment has identification 0x1234
Three fragments are created at R and the IP header of the second fragment has identification 0x1234
Three fragments are created at R and the IP header of the third fragment has 'More Fragment' (MF) bit set to 0

sourceQuestion:
Consider the three-way handshake mechanism followed during TCP connection establishment between hosts P and Q. Let X and Y be two random 32-bit starting sequence numbers chosen by P and Q respectively. Suppose P sends a TCP connection request message to Q with a TCP segment having SYN bit = 1, SEQ number = X, ACK bit = 0. Suppose Q accepts the connection request. Which one of the following choices represents the information present in the TCP segment header that is sent by Q to P?

A. SYN bit = 1, SEQ number = X + 1, ACK bit = 0, ACK number = Y, FIN bit = 0
B. SYN bit = 1, SEQ number = Y, ACK bit = 1, ACK number = X + 1, FIN bit = 0
C. SYN bit = 1, SEQ number = Y, ACK bit = 1, ACK number = X, FIN bit = 1
D. SYN bit = 1, SEQ number = X + 1, ACK bit = 1, ACK number = Y, FIN bit = 0
sourceAnswer: B. SYN bit = 1, SEQ number = Y, ACK bit = 1, ACK number = X + 1, FIN bit = 0

parsed.stem: Consider the three-way handshake mechanism followed during TCP connection establishment between hosts P and Q. Let X and Y be two random 32-bit starting sequence numbers chosen by P and Q respectively. Suppose P sends a TCP connection request message to Q with a TCP segment having SYN bit = 1, SEQ number = X, ACK bit = 0. Suppose Q accepts the connection request. Which one of the following choices represents the information present in the TCP segment header that is sent by Q to P?

options.text: SYN bit = 1, SEQ number = X + 1, ACK bit = 0, ACK number = Y, FIN bit = 0
SYN bit = 1, SEQ number = Y, ACK bit = 1, ACK number = X + 1, FIN bit = 0
SYN bit = 1, SEQ number = Y, ACK bit = 1, ACK number = X, FIN bit = 1
SYN bit = 1, SEQ number = X + 1, ACK bit = 1, ACK number = Y, FIN bit = 0

sourceQuestion:
Suppose you are asked to design a new reliable byte-stream transport protocol like TCP. This protocol, named MyTCP, runs over a network with Round Trip Time of 150 milliseconds and the maximum segment lifetime of 12 minutes. Which of the following is/are valid lengths of the Sequence Number field in the MyTCP header?

A. 30 bits
B. 32 bits
C. 34 bits
D. 36 bits

parsed.stem: Suppose you are asked to design a new reliable byte-stream transport protocol like TCP. This protocol, named MyTCP, runs over a network with Round Trip Time of 150 milliseconds and the maximum segment lifetime of 12 minutes. Which of the following is/are valid lengths of the Sequence Number field in the MyTCP header?

sourceQuestion:
A TCP client P successfully establishes a connection to TCP server Q. Let N_Q denote the sequence number in the SYN-ACK sent from Q to P. Let N_P denote the acknowledgement number in the SYN-ACK from Q to P. Which of the following statements is/are CORRECT?

A. The sequence number N_Q is chosen randomly by Q
B. The sequence number N_Q is always 0 for a new connection
C. The acknowledgement number N_P is equal to N_Q
D. The acknowledgement number N_P is equal to N_Q + 1

parsed.stem: A TCP client P successfully establishes a connection to TCP server Q. Let N_Q denote the sequence number in the SYN-ACK sent from Q to P. Let N_P denote the acknowledgement number in the SYN-ACK from Q to P. Which of the following statements is/are CORRECT?

options.text: The sequence number N_Q is chosen randomly by Q
The sequence number N_Q is always 0 for a new connection
The acknowledgement number N_P is equal to N_Q
The acknowledgement number N_P is equal to N_Q + 1

sourceQuestion:
Consider the 3-way handshaking protocol for TCP connection establishment. Let the three packets exchanged during the connection establishment be denoted as P1, P2, and P3, in order. Which of the following option(s) is/are TRUE with respect to TCP header flags that are set in the packets?

A. P2 : SYN = 1, ACK = 1
B. P1 : SYN = 1, ACK = 1
C. P3 : SYN = 1, ACK = 1
D. P1 : SYN = 1

parsed.stem: Consider the 3-way handshaking protocol for TCP connection establishment. Let the three packets exchanged during the connection establishment be denoted as P1, P2, and P3, in order. Which of the following option(s) is/are TRUE with respect to TCP header flags that are set in the packets?

sourceQuestion:
Which one of the following statements is FALSE?

A. TCP guarantees a minimum communication rate
B. TCP ensures in-order delivery
C. TCP reacts to congestion by reducing sender window size
D. TCP employs retransmission to compensate for packet loss
sourceAnswer: A. TCP guarantees a minimum communication rate

parsed.stem: Which one of the following statements is FALSE?

options.text: TCP guarantees a minimum communication rate
TCP reacts to congestion by reducing sender window size
TCP employs retransmission to compensate for packet loss

sourceQuestion:
In TCP, a unique sequence number is assigned to each

A. byte
B. word
C. segment
D. message

parsed.stem: In TCP, a unique sequence number is assigned to each

sourceQuestion:
Consider the following statements about the timeout value used in TCP:

i. The timeout value is set to the RTT (Round Trip Time) measured during TCP connection establishment for the entire duration of the connection.
ii. Appropriate RTT estimation algorithm is used to set the timeout value of a TCP connection.
iii. The timeout value is set to twice the propagation delay from the sender to the receiver.

Which of the following choices is correct?

A. (i) is true, (ii) and (iii) are false
B. (ii) and (iii) are true, (i) is false
C. (i) and (iii) are true, (ii) is false
D. (i), (ii), and (iii) are false
sourceAnswer: B. (ii) and (iii) are true, (i) is false

parsed.stem:
Consider the following statements about the timeout value used in TCP:

i. The timeout value is set to the RTT (Round Trip Time) measured during TCP connection establishment for the entire duration of the connection.
ii. Appropriate RTT estimation algorithm is used to set the timeout value of a TCP connection.
iii. The timeout value is set to twice the propagation delay from the sender to the receiver.

Which of the following choices is correct?

sourceQuestion:
Consider a TCP connection in a state where there are no outstanding ACKs. The sender sends two segments back to back. The sequence numbers of the first and second segments are 230 and 290 respectively. The first segment was lost, but the second segment was correctly received by the receiver. Let X be the amount of data (in bytes) carried in the first segment, and Y be the ACK number sent by the receiver. The values of X and Y (in order) are:

A. 60 and 290
B. 230 and 291
C. 60 and 231
D. 60 and 230

The sequence numbers of the first and second segments are 230 and 290 respectively. The first segment was lost, but the second segment was correctly received by the receiver. Let X be the amount of data (in bytes) carried in the first segment, and Y be the ACK number sent by the receiver. The values of X and Y (in order) are:

sourceQuestion:
The three way handshake for TCP connection establishment is shown below.

Client: SYN ->
Server: <- SYN+ACK
Client: ACK ->

Which of the following statements are TRUE?

S1: Loss of SYN + ACK from the server will not establish a connection
S2: Loss of ACK from the client cannot establish the connection
S3: The server moves LISTEN -> SYN_RCVD -> SYN_SENT -> ESTABLISHED in the state machine on no packet loss
S4: The server moves LISTEN -> SYN_RCVD -> ESTABLISHED in the state machine on no packet loss

A. S2 and S3 only  B. S1 and S4 only  C. S2 and S4 only  D. S3 and S4 only

parsed.stem:
The three way handshake for TCP connection establishment is shown below.

Client: SYN ->
Server: <- SYN+ACK
Client: ACK ->

Which of the following statements are TRUE?

S1: Loss of SYN + ACK from the server will not establish a connection
S2: Loss of ACK from the client cannot establish the connection
S3: The server moves LISTEN -> SYN_RCVD -> SYN_SENT -> ESTABLISHED in the state machine on no packet loss
S4: The server moves LISTEN -> SYN_RCVD -> ESTABLISHED in the state machine on no packet loss

sourceQuestion:
A computer on a 10Mbps network is regulated by a token bucket. The token bucket is filled at a rate of 2Mbps. It is initially filled to capacity with 10Megabits. What is the maximum duration for which the computer can transmit at the full 10Mbps?

A. 1.6 seconds  B. 2 seconds  C. 5 seconds  D. 8 seconds

A. TCP, but not UDP
B. TCP and UDP
C. UDP, but not TCP
D. Neither TCP nor UDP

sourceQuestion:
The transport layer protocols used for real time multimedia, file transfer, DNS and email, respectively are
A. UDP, TCP, UDP and TCP
B. TCP, UDP, TCP and UDP
C. UDP, TCP, TCP and UDP
D. TCP, UDP, UDP and TCP
sourceAnswer: A. UDP, TCP, UDP and TCP

sourceQuestion:
Consider socket API on a Linux machine that supports connected UDP sockets. A connected UDP socket is a UDP socket on which connect function has already been called. Which of the following statements is/are CORRECT?
I. A connected UDP socket can be used to communicate with multiple peers simultaneously.
II. A process can successfully call connect function for an already connected UDP socket.
A. I only
B. II only
C. Both I and II
D. Neither I nor II

I. A connected UDP socket can be used to communicate with multiple peers simultaneously.
II. A process can successfully call connect function for an already connected UDP socket.

A. Y but not Z
B. Z but not Y
C. Neither Y nor Z
D. Both Y and Z

sourceQuestion:
Which of the following is correct?
A. B trees are for storing data on disk and B+ trees are for main memory.
B. Range queries are faster on B+ trees.
C. B trees are for primary indexes and B+ trees are for secondary indexes.
D. The height of a B+ tree is independent of the number of records.
sourceAnswer: B. Range queries are faster on B+ trees.

options.text: B trees are for storing data on disk and B+ trees are for main memory.
Range queries are faster on B+ trees.
B trees are for primary indexes and B+ trees are for secondary indexes.
The height of a B+ tree is independent of the number of records.

sourceQuestion:
Consider a B+ tree in which the search key is 12 bytes long, block size is 1024 bytes, record pointer is 10 bytes, and block pointer is 8 bytes. The maximum order (number of pointers) of the B+ tree index node is:
A. 50
B. 51
C. 52
D. 53

sourceQuestion:
Consider a B+ tree with fan-out equal to 3. You are given an empty B+ tree where nodes can hold up to 5 key values. Values 1, 2, ..., 10 are inserted in order.
(a) Show the tree pictorially after all 5 insertions and after all 10 insertions. Do NOT show intermediate stages.
(b) Suppose instead of splitting a node when it is full, we try to move a value to the left sibling. If no left sibling exists or it is full, split the node. Show the tree after inserting 1, 2, ..., 10 in order.
(c) In general, suppose a B+ tree node can hold a maximum of m keys. Assuming the average number of keys in each leaf-level node is about m/2, what is the average number of keys per leaf node?
A. m/2 + 1
B. (m+1)/2
C. m/2
D. 2m

parsed.stem:
Consider a B+ tree with fan-out equal to 3. You are given an empty B+ tree where nodes can hold up to 5 key values. Values 1, 2, ..., 10 are inserted in order.
(a) Show the tree pictorially after all 5 insertions and after all 10 insertions. Do NOT show intermediate stages.
(b) Suppose instead of splitting a node when it is full, we try to move a value to the left sibling. If no left sibling exists or it is full, split the node. Show the tree after inserting 1, 2, ..., 10 in order.
(c) In general, suppose a B+ tree node can hold a maximum of m keys. Assuming the average number of keys in each leaf-level node is about m/2, what is the average number of keys per leaf node?
